full_response:
Step 1.  Distribution of a single roll under the stated condition  
The ordinary distribution of the sum S of two fair dice is

2(1), 3(2), 4(3), 5(4), 6(5), 7(6), 8(5), 9(4), 10(3), 11(2), 12(1)     (numbers in parentheses are counts of the 36 ordered pairs).

The event “neither a 5 nor a sum ≥ 8 appears” restricts every roll to the set  
{2, 3, 4, 6, 7}.  The total number of favourable pairs is

1 + 2 + 3 + 5 + 6 = 17.

Because the 10 rolls are still independent, each roll Y has the conditional pmf  

P(Y = 2) = 1/17  
P(Y = 3) = 2/17  
P(Y = 4) = 3/17  
P(Y = 6) = 5/17  
P(Y = 7) = 6/17.

Step 2.  First and second moments of one roll  
E[Y] = (2·1 + 3·2 + 4·3 + 6·5 + 7·6)/17 = 92/17.  

E[Y²] = (2²·1 + 3²·2 + 4²·3 + 6²·5 + 7²·6)/17  
         = (4 + 18 + 48 + 180 + 294)/17 = 544/17 = 32.

Var[Y] = E[Y²] − (E[Y])²  
         = 32 − (92/17)²  
         = 32 − 8464/289  
         = (32·289 − 8464)/289  
         = 784/289 = (28/17)².

Hence σ² := Var[Y] = 784/289.

Step 3.  Moments of the sum of ten rolls  
Let X = Y₁ + … + Y₁₀.  Because the Yᵢ are independent and identically distributed,

E[X] = 10 · E[Y] = 10 · 92/17 = 920/17 ≈ 54.118,

Var[X] = 10 · Var[Y] = 10 · 784/289 = 7840/289.

Step 4.  Chebyshev’s inequality  
For any random variable Z,  
Pr(|Z − E[Z]| ≥ t) ≤ Var[Z]/t².

With Z = X and t = 28,

Pr(|X − E[X]| ≥ 28) ≤ Var[X]/28²
                     = (7840/289)/784
                     = 10/289
                     ≈ 0.0346.

\boxed{\Pr[\,|X - \mathbb{E}[X]| \ge 28\,] \;\le\; \dfrac{10}{289}\;\approx\;0.0346}

Confidence: 0.88
